headline: The man behind the legendary MPC, Roger Linn, stays focused with a single browser tab
summary: Roger Linn, creator of iconic drum machines, is a music tech legend. His LM-1 and LinnDrum revolutionized music production for decades. The article explores his unique focus and work habits.

headline: Here are 38 Memorial Day deals we recommend for $50 or less
summary: Discover 38 recommended Memorial Day tech deals under $50. Find significant discounts on gadgets like portable chargers and streaming devices. Affordable tech bargains are available beyond high-ticket items.

headline: Hanging out in my favorite virtual coffee shop in Tokyo
summary: The author explores the comfort of a virtual coffee shop in Tokyo. This review details an immersive digital cafe experience. It highlights the appeal of virtual spaces for relaxation.

headline: I have a new go-to browser
summary: The author reveals their new go-to web browser in "Installer No. 129." The article likely reviews the features and user experience of this browser. It guides readers to top tech and Verge-related content.

headline: Kingdom Come Dev Confirms Next Installment Is an 'Open-World RPG' Set to Launch Next Fiscal Year
summary: Warhorse Studios confirms the next Kingdom Come game will be an open-world RPG. The highly anticipated installment is slated for launch next fiscal year. Fans can expect a new expansive adventure in the series.

headline: Dead by Daylight Players React After 'Traumatizing' Cabin Stream Confirms Jason Voorhees as New Killer
summary: Jason Voorhees is confirmed as the new killer in Dead by Daylight. The reveal followed a "traumatizing" 13-hour cabin livestream. Players have awaited Jason's arrival for ten long years.

headline: Resident Evil Movie Director Says Fans Would Be 'Bummed' If He 'Just Did the Story of the Games'
summary: Resident Evil movie director Zach Cregger explains his creative choices. He acknowledges fan desire for game-accurate adaptations. Cregger defends telling his own story, despite potential fan disappointment.

headline: Vampyros Lesbos/She Killed in Ecstasy Review
summary: A review covers the cult classic films "Vampyros Lesbos" and "She Killed in Ecstasy." These two Jess Franco works receive a stunning 4K restoration. The article highlights the visual quality and significance of the re-releases.

headline: Iran 'getting a lot closer' to agreement with US, Trump says
summary: Donald Trump suggests Iran is nearing an agreement with the US. Tehran confirms progress in talks, signaling a potential breakthrough. Initial discussions reportedly do not include nuclear weapons.

headline: France suspends extra EU border checks at Dover after hours-long queues
summary: France suspends extra EU border checks at Dover. The decision follows hours-long queues experienced by travelers. Soaring temperatures exacerbated the severe port delays.

headline: Hottest day of year as 30.5C recorded and temperatures set to soar
summary: The UK records its hottest day of the year at 30.5C. Temperatures are predicted to continue soaring across regions. Amber heat health alerts are issued for parts of England.

headline: 'Shameful' more spent on benefits than jobs for young people, says Milburn
summary: Alan Milburn criticizes higher spending on benefits than youth employment. He calls for welfare system reforms to tackle youth unemployment. A significant number of young people are not in work or education.
